comment_text.sn42.3:1.1: A “warrior” (yodhājīva) is a professional soldier. I have taken the liberty of using the name “Dustin” in honor of Dustin Cheah, my sponsor while making this translation in Qi Mei, Taiwan. Dustin (literally “Thor’s stone”) has the connotation of “valiant fighter”. | Together with the following discourses, these make up three of the four branches of the military: foot soldiers, elephant marshals, and cavalry. Sanskrit dictionaries acknowledge grāmaṇī as a military officer as well as a village headman.
comment_text.sn42.3:2.7: Another instance of kamma at death influencing rebirth is found at MN 97:38.7, where Dhanañjāni the corrupt tax collector has a deathbed conversion and goes to heaven. In Theravada circles today, it is commonly held that one’s mental state at death determines one’s rebirth. These examples show that this is true only in exceptional cases where one is actively making strong good or bad kamma at the time of death.

root_text.sn42.3:1.1: Atha kho yodhājīvo gāmaṇi yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā …pe… ekamantaṁ nisinno kho yodhājīvo gāmaṇi bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn42.3:1.2: “sutaṁ metaṁ, bhante, pubbakānaṁ ācariyapācariyānaṁ yodhājīvānaṁ bhāsamānānaṁ:
root_text.sn42.3:1.3: ‘yo so yodhājīvo saṅgāme ussahati vāyamati, tamenaṁ ussahantaṁ vāyamantaṁ pare hananti pariyāpādenti, so kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā parajitānaṁ devānaṁ sahabyataṁ upapajjatī’ti.
root_text.sn42.3:1.5: “Alaṁ, gāmaṇi, tiṭṭhatetaṁ; mā maṁ etaṁ pucchī”ti.
root_text.sn42.3:1.7: tatiyampi kho yodhājīvo gāmaṇi bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn42.3:1.8: “sutaṁ metaṁ, bhante, pubbakānaṁ ācariyapācariyānaṁ yodhājīvānaṁ bhāsamānānaṁ:
root_text.sn42.3:1.9: ‘yo so yodhājīvo saṅgāme ussahati vāyamati, tamenaṁ ussahantaṁ vāyamantaṁ pare hananti pariyāpādenti, so kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā parajitānaṁ devānaṁ sahabyataṁ upapajjatī’ti.
root_text.sn42.3:2.2: ‘alaṁ, gāmaṇi, tiṭṭhatetaṁ; mā maṁ etaṁ pucchī’ti.
root_text.sn42.3:2.4: Yo so, gāmaṇi, yodhājīvo saṅgāme ussahati vāyamati, tassa taṁ cittaṁ pubbe gahitaṁ dukkaṭaṁ duppaṇihitaṁ:
root_text.sn42.3:2.5: ‘ime sattā haññantu vā bajjhantu vā ucchijjantu vā vinassantu vā mā vā ahesuṁ iti vā’ti.
root_text.sn42.3:2.6: Tamenaṁ ussahantaṁ vāyamantaṁ pare hananti pariyāpādenti;
root_text.sn42.3:2.7: so kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā parajito nāma nirayo tattha upapajjatīti.
root_text.sn42.3:2.8: Sace kho panassa evaṁ diṭṭhi hoti:
root_text.sn42.3:2.9: ‘yo so yodhājīvo saṅgāme ussahati vāyamati tamenaṁ ussahantaṁ vāyamantaṁ pare hananti pariyāpādenti, so kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā parajitānaṁ devānaṁ sahabyataṁ upapajjatī’ti, sāssa hoti micchādiṭṭhi.
root_text.sn42.3:2.10: Micchādiṭṭhikassa kho panāhaṁ, gāmaṇi, purisapuggalassa dvinnaṁ gatīnaṁ aññataraṁ gatiṁ vadāmi—
root_text.sn42.3:3.1: Evaṁ vutte, yodhājīvo gāmaṇi parodi, assūni pavattesi.
root_text.sn42.3:3.3: ‘alaṁ, gāmaṇi, tiṭṭhatetaṁ; mā maṁ etaṁ pucchī’”ti.
root_text.sn42.3:3.4: “Nāhaṁ, bhante, etaṁ rodāmi yaṁ maṁ bhagavā evamāha;
root_text.sn42.3:3.5: api cāhaṁ, bhante, pubbakehi ācariyapācariyehi yodhājīvehi dīgharattaṁ nikato vañcito paluddho:
root_text.sn42.3:3.6: ‘yo so yodhājīvo saṅgāme ussahati vāyamati, tamenaṁ ussahantaṁ vāyamantaṁ pare hananti pariyāpādenti, so kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā parajitānaṁ devānaṁ sahabyataṁ upapajjatī’”ti.

translation_text.sn42.3:1.1: Then Dustin the warrior chief went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him:
translation_text.sn42.3:1.2: “Sir, I have heard that the warriors of the past who were tutors of tutors said:
translation_text.sn42.3:1.3: ‘Suppose a warrior, while striving and struggling in battle, is killed and finished off by his foes. When his body breaks up, after death, he’s reborn in the company of the gods of the fallen.’
translation_text.sn42.3:1.4: What does the Buddha say about this?”
translation_text.sn42.3:1.5: “Enough, chief, let it be. Don’t ask me that.”
translation_text.sn42.3:1.7: And for a third time Dustin the warrior chief said to the Buddha:
translation_text.sn42.3:1.8: “Sir, I have heard that the warriors of the past who were tutors of tutors said:
translation_text.sn42.3:1.9: ‘Suppose a warrior, while striving and struggling in battle, is killed and finished off by his foes. When his body breaks up, after death, he’s reborn in the company of the gods of the fallen.’
translation_text.sn42.3:1.10: What does the Buddha say about this?”
translation_text.sn42.3:2.1: “Clearly, chief, I’m not getting through to you when I say:
translation_text.sn42.3:2.2: ‘Enough, chief, let it be. Don’t ask me that.’
translation_text.sn42.3:2.4: When a warrior strives and struggles in battle, their mind is already low, degraded, and misdirected as they think:
translation_text.sn42.3:2.5: ‘May these sentient beings be killed, slaughtered, slain, destroyed, or annihilated!’
translation_text.sn42.3:2.6: His foes kill him and finish him off, and
translation_text.sn42.3:2.7: when his body breaks up, after death, he’s reborn in the hell called ‘The Fallen’.
translation_text.sn42.3:2.8: But if you have such a view:
translation_text.sn42.3:2.9: ‘Suppose a warrior, while striving and struggling in battle, is killed and finished off by his foes. When his body breaks up, after death, he’s reborn in the company of the gods of the fallen.’ This is your wrong view.
translation_text.sn42.3:2.10: An individual with wrong view is reborn in one of two places, I say:
translation_text.sn42.3:3.1: When he said this, Dustin the warrior chief cried and burst out in tears.
translation_text.sn42.3:3.2: “This is what I didn’t get through to you when I said:
translation_text.sn42.3:3.3: ‘Enough, chief, let it be. Don’t ask me that.’”
translation_text.sn42.3:3.4: “Sir, I’m not crying because of what the Buddha said.
translation_text.sn42.3:3.5: But sir, for a long time I’ve been cheated, tricked, and deceived by the warriors of the past who were tutors of tutors, who said:
translation_text.sn42.3:3.6: ‘Suppose a warrior, while striving and struggling in battle, is killed and finished off by his foes. When his body breaks up, after death, he’s reborn in the company of the gods of the fallen.’
translation_text.sn42.3:3.8: From this day forth, may the Buddha remember me as a lay follower who has gone for refuge for life.”
